2605.23595.title: Cost-Effective Model Evaluation with Meta-Learning
2605.23595.paper_summary: The rapid growth of machine learning has produced an ever-expanding ecosystem of models, making it increasingly challenging to verify the reliability of newly released models on unseen, unlabeled data. Conventional evaluation pipelines depend on expensive annotation, repeated fine-tuning, or narrow assumptions that fail to transfer across model families. We present MetaEvaluator, a cost-effective, model-agnostic framework for rapid, label-free assessment of unseen models spanning diverse architectures and modalities. MetaEvaluator leverages meta-learning over a pool of reference models to obtain a transferable initialization, enabling accurate evaluation of new models while amortizing cost across the pool and removing the need for per-model retraining. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first model-agnostic framework capable of evaluating new models on entirely unlabeled datasets. Extensive experiments show that MetaEvaluator produces stable and accurate performance estimates at substantially reduced cost compared to conventional approaches, making scalable benchmarking of emerging models on unlabeled data practical.
2605.23595.paper_summary_zh: 機器學習的快速成長產生了一個不斷擴大的模型生態系統，使得在未見過的、未標記的數據上驗證新發布模型的可靠性變得越來越具挑戰性。傳統的評估流程依賴於昂貴的標註、重複的微調或無法跨模型系列轉移的狹隘假設。我們提出了MetaEvaluator，一個成本效益高、模型無關的框架，用於快速、無標籤地評估涵蓋多種架構和模態的未見模型。MetaEvaluator利用參考模型池上的元學習來獲取可轉移的初始化，使得能夠準確評估新模型，同時在池中攤銷成本，消除對每個模型重新訓練的需求。據我們所知，這是第一個能在完全未標記數據集上評估新模型的模型無關框架。廣泛的實驗顯示，與傳統方法相比，MetaEvaluator在顯著降低成本的同時，產生穩定且準確的性能估計，使得在未標記數據上對新興模型的可擴展基準測試變得實際可行。
2605.23595.author: Trinh Pham et.al.
2605.23595.authors: Trinh Pham, Viet Huynh, Hongzhi Yin, Quoc Viet Hung Nguyen, Thanh Tam Nguyen
